title: Distributed lock without TTL or safe release — deadlock risk
expected_feedback: Standard: Two problems — (1) lock has no TTL (expiration=0), so if the holder crashes or hangs, the lock is held forever, permanently blocking all subsequent payment processing for this order. (2) DEL without ownership check: if the lock expired and another process acquired it, this DEL releases someone else's lock, causing concurrent execution. Fix: set TTL (e.g., 10s), use a unique token as lock value, and release with Lua CAS script that checks token before deleting.
violated_rule: Distributed locks have TTL and safe CAS release
